translations.translation:
Dong He (Youzai)
董和 (幼宰)
Lifespan Unknown
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]

byZL181
onMarch 1st, 2025

Dong He, styledYouzai, was fromZhijiang,Nan commandery. His ancestors were originally fromJiangzhou,Ba commandery.
During the last stages ofHan,[Dong] Heled his clan and kin to move west. TheGovernorofYi province,Liu Zhang, had him serve asChiefofNiubi[牛鞞長],AJiangyuan[江原長], andPrefectofChengdu[成都令]. The lands of Shu [蜀] were rich and abundant, and the social customs of the time were those of extravagance and wastefulness. The households of businesspeople wore the dresses of lords and consumed exquisite food. Whether for marriages or funerals, entire family properties would be spent on such.[Dong] Heled as a model of frugality, wearing poor clothes and eating coarse food.1He curbed and controlled those who were overstepping and acting brazenly2by formulating regulations against such, and wherever he was, he would always transform the customs into one of goodness. They were in awe of him and did not violate [laws]. Those who were mighty and powerful in the counties feared the stringent laws of[Dong] Heand persuaded[Liu] Zhangto transfer[Dong] Heto becomeChief CommandantofBadong Dependent States[巴東屬國都尉]. The officials, commoners, old, and weak who brought each other to beg for[Dong] Heto remain numbered in the thousands.[Liu] Zhangheeded them and let him stay for two years, then had him promoted to serve asGrand AdministratorofYizhou. He was pure and economical as he was before. He handled matters with the Man barbarians and strived hard in advocating sincere intentions, and so the lands of the south loved and trustedhim.•••
Here, “wearing poor clothes and eating coarse food” [惡衣蔬食] is not too unsimilar to what theAnalectsencouraged with its “wearing bad clothes and eating bad food” [惡衣惡食].↩
Here, “overstepping and acting brazenly” [踰僭] refers to people who acted beyond their status and even acted in the name of the authorities.↩
[Bi [鞞]] is pronounced Bi [髀].
When theFormer Lordstabilized Shu [蜀], he summoned[Dong] Heto serve asHousehold General who Controls the Army. He and theGeneral who Directs the Military,Zhuge Liang, would both manage bureau affairs of theGeneral of Chariots and CavalryandGeneral of the Left3as proxies, and they would present the acceptable and discontinue the unacceptable. They thus shared a joyful friendship. Ever since[Dong] Heoccupied office and received salary, he governed distant places outside and undertook critical matters inside: this he did for over twenty years. When he died, his household lacked even smallsavings.•••
These were titles held byLiu Bei. This passage is another way of saying that many affairs were entrusted toDong HeandZhuge Liang.↩
***
When[Zhuge] Lianglater served asAssisting Chancellor, he gave this instruction to the officials:
“Those who act in posts should congregate the opinions of the masses and expand loyalty and advantages. Supposing if we avoid minute grievances and find it difficult to have differing opinions with each other, then our deficiencies and disadvantages will grow. To have differing opinions and achieving the middle ground thereof would be like casting away worn shoes and capturing pearls and jade! Whereas the minds of people suffer from being unable to do such to the fullest, onlyXuYuanzhiwas unperturbed in undertaking such. Furthermore,DongYouzaiacted in a post for seven years, and whenever a matter was not comprehensive, he would go back to it as many as ten times, then come to inform of it. If one could emulate even one-tenths ofYuanzhiand the deep diligence ofYouzai, and be steadfast to the nation, then I may err less!”
He also said:
“In the past, I had metZhouping, hearing my advantages and disadvantages from him several times, and later, I metYuanzhi, receiving enlightening guidance from him often. When I consulted matters withYouzaiin the past, he would always speak to the fullest extent, and when I discussed matters withWeidu, he would offer multiple remonstrations. Although I am willful and shallow, being unable to accept [their suggestions] in entirety, I had always been friendly with those four masters throughout, which was also sufficient enough for them to display their lack of hesitation in giving me their candid words.”
His remembrance of[Dong] Hewassuch.B
ThisWeidu, surnamed Hu and forenamed Ji, was fromYiyang[義陽]. He served asMaster of Recordsfor[Zhuge] Liangand rendered absolute loyalty, which he was praised and recorded for. When[Zhuge] Liangdied, he served as Army Organizer of the Center and commanded several armies. He was enfeoffed asDistrict Marquisof Chengyang [成陽亭侯], then promoted toArmy Supervisor of the CenterandGeneral of the Front. He supervisedHanzhong[漢中] and wieldedacting tally, and he served asInspectorofYan province[兖州刺史]. He attained the rank of RightGeneral of Coursers. The younger brother of[Hu] Jiwas[Hu] Bo, who served asCommandant of the Chang River EncampmentsandSecretariat Minister.
